seoTitle: How to Add Attachments When Emailing Invoices From QuickBooks
metaDescription: Attach receipts, contracts, or supporting documents to invoice emails in QuickBooks for clearer client communication and faster payments.
intro: Attaching supporting documents like receipts or contracts to an invoice email helps clients get everything they need in one place, which can speed up payment and reduce back-and-forth questions. This guide covers adding attachments to invoice emails in QuickBooks.

steps.heading: Open the invoice you want to email
steps.body: Navigate to the specific invoice in QuickBooks that you're preparing to send to a client.

steps.heading: Locate the attachment option
steps.body: Find the feature within the invoicing screen that lets you attach a file to the outgoing email.

steps.heading: Upload the supporting document
steps.body: Add the relevant file, such as a receipt, contract, or product specification, to accompany the invoice.

steps.heading: Review before sending
steps.body: Double-check that the correct attachment is included and named clearly before sending the invoice email.

steps.heading: Send the invoice with attachment
steps.body: Complete the send process so the client receives both the invoice and the supporting documentation together.

faq.question: What types of files can I attach to a QuickBooks invoice email?
faq.answer: Common attachments include receipts, contracts, and product specifications, though supported file types can depend on your QuickBooks setup.

faq.question: Is there a file size limit for attachments in QuickBooks?
faq.answer: QuickBooks generally applies limits on attachment size, so very large files may need to be compressed or shared separately.

faq.question: Which industries benefit most from attaching documents to invoices?
faq.answer: Industries where documentation matters most, like construction, legal services, and consulting, often benefit from attaching supporting files.

faq.question: Can I attach multiple files to a single invoice email?
faq.answer: Depending on your QuickBooks setup, you may be able to attach more than one file, which is useful when several documents need to accompany an invoice.
